title: crataegus holmesiana var. tardipes
intro: Crataegus holmesiana var. tardipes, also known as the late-flowering hawthorn, is a variant of the Crataegus holmesiana plant, belonging to the Rosaceae family. This shrub or small tree is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its cardiovascular benefits, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Crataegus holmesiana var. tardipes include improved heart health, reduced blood pressure, and enhanced antioxidant defenses, making it a popular herbal remedy for cardiovascular diseases. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in various forms, including tinctures, teas, and capsules, which can be used to support cardiovascular health and overall well-being.
